summary:
Claim: The lateral aspect of the tongue is drained by posterior lymphatic vessels, not ventral ones.  
Evidence: - Posterior lymphatics run along the lateral/side walls of the tongue and drain into posterior cervical nodes. - Marginal sinuses cover gingival margins but lack extensive coverage of lateral surfaces.  
Answer: C

Answer: C. Answer choice: C
